steps.title: Combine fractions with common denominator
steps.content:
Notice that $\frac{x^{2}}{\sqrt{x^{2}-c^{2}}}$ and $\frac{c^{2}}{\sqrt{x^{2}-c^{2}}}$ share the same denominator. Move the second fraction to the left side:

$$\frac{x^{2}}{\sqrt{x^{2}-c^{2}}} - \frac{c^{2}}{\sqrt{x^{2}-c^{2}}} = 39$$

Combine into a single fraction:

$$\frac{x^{2} - c^{2}}{\sqrt{x^{2}-c^{2}}} = 39$$

steps.title: Use substitution to simplify the radical
steps.content:
Let $u = \sqrt{x^{2}-c^{2}}$, which means $u^{2} = x^{2} - c^{2}$. Substitute into the equation:

$$\frac{u^{2}}{u} = 39$$

Cancel the $u$ in numerator and denominator:

$$u = 39$$

steps.title: Solve for x and identify the solution
steps.content:
Back-substitute $u = 39$ into $u = \sqrt{x^{2}-c^{2}}$:

$$\sqrt{x^{2}-c^{2}} = 39$$

Square both sides:

$$x^{2} - c^{2} = 1521$$

Since $1521 = 39^{2}$:

$$x^{2} = c^{2} + 39^{2}$$

Therefore:

$$x = \pm\sqrt{c^{2} + 39^{2}}$$

The negative solution is $-\sqrt{c^{2} + 39^{2}}$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
